title: Token Bucket Rate Limiting Algorithm
description: Implements token bucket algorithm to control API request rate with burst capability
code:
CLASS TokenBucket
 capacity: Integer // Maximum tokens in bucket
 refillRate: Integer // Tokens added per second
 tokens: Float // Current token count
 lastRefillTime: Timestamp

 FUNCTION allowRequest(requestCost: Integer) : Boolean
 currentTime = NOW()
 timePassed = currentTime - lastRefillTime
 tokens = MIN(capacity, tokens + (timePassed * refillRate))
 lastRefillTime = currentTime
 
 IF tokens >= requestCost THEN
 tokens = tokens - requestCost
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION getRetryAfter() : Integer
 tokensNeeded = 1 - tokens
 RETURN CEILING(tokensNeeded / refillRate)
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
explanation: Token bucket allows burst traffic up to bucket capacity while maintaining average rate. Useful for APIs that experience burst patterns.

title: Circuit Breaker Pattern Implementation
description: Prevents cascading failures by monitoring API call health
code:
CLASS CircuitBreaker
 failureThreshold: Integer = 5
 recoveryTimeout: Integer = 30
 successThreshold: Integer = 2
 state: String = "CLOSED"
 failureCount: Integer = 0
 successCount: Integer = 0
 lastFailureTime: Timestamp

 FUNCTION execute(apiCall: Function) : Result
 IF state == "OPEN" THEN
 IF NOW() - lastFailureTime > recoveryTimeout THEN
 state = "HALF-OPEN"
 ELSE
 THROW CircuitBreakerOpenException
 END IF
 END IF
 
 TRY
 result = apiCall()
 onSuccess()
 RETURN result
 CATCH Exception
 onFailure()
 THROW Exception
 END TRY
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION onSuccess()
 IF state == "HALF-OPEN" THEN
 successCount = successCount + 1
 IF successCount >= successThreshold THEN
 state = "CLOSED"
 failureCount = 0
 successCount = 0
 END IF
 ELSE
 failureCount = 0
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION onFailure()
 failureCount = failureCount + 1
 lastFailureTime = NOW()
 IF failureCount >= failureThreshold THEN
 state = "OPEN"
 END IF
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
explanation: Circuit breaker prevents system overload by failing fast when downstream services are unhealthy, allowing them time to recover.

title: API Response Caching with TTL
description: Implements cache management with time-based expiration
code:
CLASS APICache
 cacheStore: HashMap<Key, CacheEntry>
 defaultTTL: Integer = 300 // seconds

 STRUCT CacheEntry
 value: Any
 expiryTime: Timestamp
 hitCount: Integer
 END STRUCT

 FUNCTION get(key: String) : Any
 entry = cacheStore.get(key)
 
 IF entry == NULL THEN
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
 
 IF NOW() > entry.expiryTime THEN
 cacheStore.remove(key)
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
 
 entry.hitCount = entry.hitCount + 1
 RETURN entry.value
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION set(key: String, value: Any, ttl: Integer)
 entry = CacheEntry()
 entry.value = value
 entry.expiryTime = NOW() + (ttl OR defaultTTL)
 entry.hitCount = 0
 cacheStore.put(key, entry)
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION evictExpired()
 FOR EACH key, entry IN cacheStore
 IF NOW() > entry.expiryTime THEN
 cacheStore.remove(key)
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
explanation: Cache with TTL prevents stale data and manages memory by automatically removing expired entries. TTL should be tuned based on data volatility.

title: Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter
description: Implements leaky bucket algorithm for steady-rate request processing
code:
CLASS LeakyBucket
 capacity: Integer // Maximum bucket size
 leakRate: Integer // Requests processed per second
 currentLevel: Integer // Current requests in bucket
 lastLeakTime: Timestamp

 FUNCTION addRequest(request: Request) : Boolean
 leak()
 
 IF currentLevel < capacity THEN
 currentLevel = currentLevel + 1
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE // Request rejected
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION leak()
 currentTime = NOW()
 timePassed = currentTime - lastLeakTime
 leaked = timePassed * leakRate
 currentLevel = MAX(0, currentLevel - leaked)
 lastLeakTime = currentTime
 END FUNCTION

 FUNCTION getQueuePosition() : Integer
 leak()
 RETURN currentLevel
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
explanation: Leaky bucket processes requests at a constant rate, smoothing out burst traffic. Ideal for systems that need predictable processing rates.

title: API Auto-Scaling Decision Logic
description: Dynamic capacity adjustment based on traffic patterns
code:
FUNCTION evaluateScaling(
 currentInstances: Integer,
 metrics: APIMetrics
) : ScalingDecision
 avgCPU = metrics.getAverageCPU()
 avgMemory = metrics.getAverageMemory()
 requestQueueSize = metrics.getQueueSize()
 errorRate = metrics.getErrorRate()
 
 // Scale out conditions
 IF avgCPU > 80 OR avgMemory > 85 THEN
 IF requestQueueSize > 100 THEN
 RETURN ScaleOut(min(4, currentInstances * 2))
 END IF
 RETURN ScaleOut(min(2, currentInstances + 1))
 END IF
 
 // Scale in conditions (conservative)
 IF avgCPU < 30 AND avgMemory < 40 THEN
 IF requestQueueSize == 0 AND errorRate < 1 THEN
 RETURN ScaleIn(max(1, currentInstances - 1))
 END IF
 END IF
 
 RETURN NoScale()
END FUNCTION

// Metrics collection
FUNCTION collectAPIMetrics(
 requests: List<Request>,
 windowSeconds: Integer = 60
) : APIMetrics
 recentRequests = requests.last(windowSeconds)
 
 return APIMetrics(
 avgCPU: recentRequests.avg(r => r.cpuUsage),
 avgMemory: recentRequests.avg(r => r.memoryUsage),
 requestQueueSize: getCurrentQueueSize(),
 errorRate: recentRequests.count(r => r.isError) / recentRequests.size
 )
END FUNCTION
explanation: Auto-scaling dynamically adjusts capacity based on real metrics. Scale-out handles increased load; scale-in saves costs during low traffic. Should include hysteresis to prevent thrashing.
